title: AI Model Governance Briefing Scope

timeframe.note: Only developments published or updated within the last 12 months from today.

entities_in_scope: EU: European Commission, Council and Parliament (for implementation updates), EU AI Office, national competent authorities, ENISA, EDPB, EDPB/EDPS joint work where applicable

US: White House/OSTP, OMB, NIST and the NIST AI Safety Institute (AISI), FTC, DOJ (Antitrust/Civil), DHS and CISA, relevant Congressional committees, state attorneys general and state legislatures when the action clearly affects foundation models
topics_emphasis: Obligations on foundation models / general-purpose AI under the EU AI Act (e.g., codes of practice, systemic risk management, model evaluations, technical documentation, incident reporting, content provenance)
US federal actions affecting foundation models (e.g., NIST/AISI testing and safety guidance, OMB memos and implementation timelines, FTC policy/enforcement actions, DHS/CISA directives or advisories)
Model safety evaluation and red-teaming expectations for high-impact systems
Content provenance (watermarking/origin signals), disclosure and transparency obligations
Compute/reporting thresholds, risk classification criteria, and reporting channels
Security and abuse prevention requirements (e.g., protections against misuse and model exfiltration)
Open-source model considerations, carve-outs, or obligations specific to foundation models
out_of_scope: Corporate marketing blogs that do not reflect formal policy or regulator guidance
Pure academic theory without clear regulatory implications
Developments older than the specified timeframe unless briefly cited for minimal context

deliverable_expectations.tone: Executive-friendly and action-oriented; avoid unnecessary technical depth
deliverable_expectations.citations: Use numeric inline citations like [#] that map to the 1-based index of output/sources.json

questions_to_answer: What new or updated obligations apply to providers of foundation models in the EU and the US?
What are the key dates and timelines (phased implementation, effective dates, public comment windows)?
Where do EU and US approaches align or diverge and how does that affect global launch plans?
What practical steps should we take in the next quarter to remain compliant?
What open questions remain pending secondary guidance or further rulemaking?
notes_for_research: Prioritize official regulator/government domains and reputable outlets/think tanks.
Flag contradictions across sources and propose a reasoned reconciliation.
